title: Extract test orchestration into pure captureTestRun() for testable learning loop
context: The test-learning loop (capture failures→bugs, resolve passing tests, promote recurrences→constraints) was embedded in CLI handler code mixed with console I/O, making it impossible to unit-test the core logic without mocking I/O.
decision: Refactor test capture logic into captureTestRun(store, root, {report, status, cmd, output}) — a pure function returning {results, fixed, fallback}. CLI handler now calls it and formats output separately. This cleanly separates orchestration from I/O, enabling comprehensive unit testing of the entire loop.
consequences: Test-learning loop now fully unit-tested with 5 cases (failing→bug, passing→resolved, unrecognized+fail→fallback, unrecognized+success→no bug, recurrence→constraint)
CLI code simplifies: orchestration logic moves to synthesize.ts, CLI becomes display-only (console.log results from pure function)
Fallback behavior (coarse bug from output tail) now explicit and testable
Loop logic becomes reusable outside CLI (testable primitive for other integrations)
Each learning stage (capture, resolve, promote) independently verifiable without end-to-end test setup
alternatives_rejected: Keep logic in CLI, mock console with unit tests — mixes concerns, brittle coupling to I/O
Cover via end-to-end CLI tests only — slow, brittle, harder to test edge cases (synthetic repos, malformed output)
Leave loop untested — original state, easy to regress the critical bug/constraint/resolve loop
